data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T190856-167ae456/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T190856-167ae456/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T190856-167ae456/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T190856-167ae456/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T190856-167ae456/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-fcdbc731">
    <title>prompts: tighten shared blocks; add FEAT-022 guardrail-list comment block; add invariant tests</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Both execute-bead prompt strings (Claude: 1078 words, Agent: 1020 words) contain prose paragraphs that consume more tokens than equivalent bullet checklists. 19 load-bearing guardrails are embedded in prose without explicit enumeration, making omission hard to detect. No invariant tests verify that selectors and the missing-governing path work correctly.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package): the shared blocks (extracted by ddx-e2171a2c, a dep) are prose paragraphs averaging ~25 words each. Equivalent checklists would cut to ~10 words per guardrail.
- No FEAT-022-referencing comment block exists that enumerates all 19 guardrails inline.
- No invariant tests exist for prompt selectors (which harness-specific block is chosen) or missing-governing path (behavior when governing doc is absent).
- ddx-e2171a2c (shared block extraction, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Tighten shared blocks: convert prose paragraphs to bullet checklists targeting >= 30% word reduction on both Claude and Agent variants (~400 words saved on Claude, ~450 on Agent).
- Verify all 19 guardrails preserved: enumerate each guardrail in a comment or test.
- Add FEAT-022-referencing comment block listing all 19 guardrails inline (with FEAT-022 cross-reference).
- Add invariant tests:
  - TestPromptSelector_CorrectBlock: verifies each harness gets its specific block.
  - TestPromptSelector_MissingGoverning: verifies missing-governing path uses fallback or errors predictably.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing prompt semantics or dropping any of the 19 guardrails.
- Adding new guardrails (those go in the rubric update).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Both prompt variants (Claude + Agent) word count reduced by >= 30% from baseline.
2. All 19 guardrails preserved: TestPromptGuardrails_AllPresent enumerates and asserts each.
3. FEAT-022 comment block exists listing all 19 guardrails with cross-reference.
4. TestPromptSelector_CorrectBlock verifies each harness gets its specific block.
5. TestPromptSelector_MissingGoverning verifies missing-governing path behavior.
6. Existing eval suite passes (golden output unchanged beyond word-reduction reformatting).
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:12, area:agent, kind:feature, triage:no-changes-unverified</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a61bf8ee" base-rev="94199ed85cc08c2c0c7a1aaf359cf9feced008f0" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T190856-167ae456"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
